name: spec-kit-claude-code-workflow
description: A conceptual skill for guiding the Spec-Kit + Claude Code development workflow
capabilities: Define repository-wide guidelines using multi-folder CLAUDE.md files
Create standardized specification templates and validation rules
Guide Claude Code to align implementations with specification requirements
Establish iteration cycles for specification and prompt refinement
Maintain traceability between specifications and code artifacts
Implement quality assurance frameworks for specs and implementations
author: Claude
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only YAML frontmatter and markdown guidance for development workflow. No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or command execution capabilities. All 15 static findings are false positives from pattern-matching on benign documentation text.
valueStatement: Developers struggle to maintain consistency between specifications and implementations across multi-folder repositories. This skill provides a structured framework for specification-driven development that integrates seamlessly with Claude Code assistance.
userTitle: Guide Spec-Kit + Claude Code Development Workflow
limitations: This is a conceptual guidance skill without executable code
Requires existing repository structure to be effective
Does not generate code or modify files automatically
Team commitment to specification-driven approach required

useCases.title: Establish Project Standards
useCases.description: Define consistent development patterns and specification formats across multi-folder repositories

useCases.title: Improve Code Quality
useCases.description: Ensure implementations trace back to clear specifications with proper validation checkpoints

useCases.title: Enhance Claude Code Context
useCases.description: Provide Claude Code with structured specifications to improve code generation accuracy

promptTemplates.title: Set Up Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the Spec-Kit workflow skill to establish CLAUDE.md files in the root and subfolders of my project. Create guidelines for specification-driven development.

promptTemplates.title: Create Specification
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through creating a feature specification using Spec-Kit templates. Include success criteria and validation rules.

promptTemplates.title: Validate Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the Spec-Kit workflow, validate that my implementation matches the specification requirements. Identify gaps and missing test cases.

promptTemplates.title: Refine Process
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze our current Spec-Kit workflow. Suggest improvements to our specification templates and Claude Code integration patterns.

bestPractices: Create specifications before implementation to establish clear success criteria
Use multi-folder CLAUDE.md files to enable inheritance while allowing folder-specific overrides
Maintain bidirectional traceability between specifications and code changes
antiPatterns: Skipping specification creation and starting with code immediately
Creating specifications without testable acceptance criteria
Ignoring specification changes during active implementation phases

faq.question: What is Spec-Kit?
faq.answer: Spec-Kit is a framework for specification-driven development that structures how you document and validate project requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my files?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance only. You apply the recommendations manually or use them to guide Claude Code.

faq.question: What CLAUDE.md files do I need?
faq.answer: Create a root CLAUDE.md for project-wide rules and optionally add folder-specific files for module-level conventions.

faq.question: How do I measure specification quality?
faq.answer: Check that specifications are complete, testable, implementable, and include clear success criteria and acceptance tests.

faq.question: Can I use this with existing projects?
faq.answer: Yes, analyze your current structure first, then introduce Spec-Kit incrementally starting with new features.

faq.question: How does this compare to traditional agile?
faq.answer: It complements agile by providing more rigorous specification discipline while maintaining iterative development cycles.
